Description: Properly structure composer.json with all necessary sections for a PHP project
Do: Define name, description, require, require-dev, autoload with PSR-4, scripts
Dont: Leave composer.json with minimal config. Don't commit composer.lock (for apps)

Description: Map namespaces to directories using PSR-4 standard for automatic class loading
Do: One namespace root per source directory. Match directory structure to namespace hierarchy
Dont: Use classmap for application code. Don't mix PSR-0 and PSR-4

Description: Use appropriate version constraints to balance stability and updates
Do: Use ^ (caret) for most packages (allows minor+patch updates). Pin major versions
Dont: Use * (wildcard) or dev-main in production. Don't use exact versions unless required

Description: composer.lock ensures deterministic installs. Behavior differs for apps vs libraries
Do: For apps: commit composer.lock. For libraries: gitignore composer.lock
Dont: For apps: don't gitignore composer.lock. Don't run composer update in production
Example:
# Application workflow:
composer install  # Uses composer.lock (deterministic)
# Only run update when intentionally upgrading:
composer update laravel/framework  # Updates specific package
composer install --no-dev  # Production

# Library workflow:
# .gitignore includes composer.lock
composer update  # Always get latest compatible versions

Description: Separate development dependencies from production dependencies
Do: Put testing, linting, debugging tools in require-dev. Use --no-dev in production
Dont: Install dev dependencies in production images

Description: Configure access to private Composer packages hosted on private registries
Do: Use Private Packagist or Satis for private packages. Use auth.json for tokens
Dont: Hardcode auth tokens in composer.json or commit auth.json to git

Description: Optimize Composer autoloading for production to avoid filesystem lookups
Do: Run composer dump-autoload -o in production. Use classmap-authoritative for max speed
Dont: Use development autoloading in production (slower filesystem lookups)
Example:
# Production deployment:
composer install --no-dev --optimize-autoloader --classmap-authoritative

# Or in composer.json:
\config": {
 "optimize-autoloader": true

Description: Specify PHP version and extension requirements in composer.json
Do: Declare required PHP version and extensions in require section. Use platform-check
Dont: Deploy without checking PHP version compatibility

Description: Define standard scripts in composer.json for CI/CD consistency
Do: Define test lint format and check scripts. Use composite scripts for full CI
Dont: Run ad-hoc commands in CI instead of standardized scripts

Description: Regularly check dependencies for known security vulnerabilities
Do: Run composer audit regularly and in CI. Use roave/security-advisories to prevent insecure installs
Dont: Ignore security advisories. Don't audit dependencies
Example:
# Check for vulnerabilities:
composer audit

# Prevent installing packages with known vulnerabilities:
composer require --dev roave/security-advisories:dev-latest

# In CI:
composer audit --format=json
# Fails if vulnerabilities found

Description: Use path repositories for monorepo setups where multiple packages live in one repository
Do: Use type:path repositories with symlink for local development
Dont: Publish and install local packages from Packagist during development

Description: Use Composer plugins for custom installation logic and workflow automation
Do: Use well-maintained plugins. Audit plugins since they run arbitrary code
Dont: Install unknown plugins that could execute malicious code
Example:
# Useful plugins:
composer require --dev phpstan/extension-installer
composer require --dev dealerdirect/phpcodesniffer-composer-installer

# Allow specific plugins:
\config": {
 "allow-plugins": {
  "phpstan/extension-installer": true

Description: Have a systematic approach to keeping dependencies updated
Do: Use Dependabot or Renovate for automated PRs. Review changelogs before major updates
Dont: Let dependencies go stale for months/years. Run composer update blindly
Example:
# GitHub Dependabot:
# .github/dependabot.yml
version: 2
updates:
  - package-ecosystem: composer
    directory: '/'
    schedule:
      interval: weekly
    open-pull-requests-limit: 10

# Manual careful update:
composer outdated
composer update --dry-run
composer update specific/package

Description: Organize namespaces to match your architectural layers and domain boundaries
Do: Follow PSR-4 with meaningful namespace hierarchy. Group by feature or layer
Dont: Use flat namespaces. Don't match namespace to directory structure

Description: Control which package stability levels are acceptable in your project
Do: Keep minimum-stability at stable (default). Use specific flags for individual packages
